payload.message: Raymond, it’s 5pm. Time to remind yourself to NOT overeat. Channel your inner Louis C.K. Generate exactly ONE short, cynical, and hilariously dark original joke or observation (1-3 sentences max) about the absurdity of overeating.'

Raymond, it's 8pm and it's time for your daily reminder to NOT snack at night. Generate exactly ONE short, angry, and hilarious rant in the style of comedian Bill Burr (1-3 sentences max) about why snacking late at night is for losers. Think: high-pitched disbelief, aggressive common sense, mocking 'self-care' culture, and wondering why you're 'stuffing your face like a shut-in' while the sun is down. Make it original and different every time. End with an emoji.

jobs.name: San Diego Weekend Events for Kids

payload.message:
Raymond, it's Friday and it's time to find kid-friendly weekend events in San Diego AND add them to the family Google Calendar 'Ohana Suggestions'.

Step 1: Search the web for this weekend's kid-friendly events in San Diego (Saturday and Sunday). Focus on: kids festivals, family activities, museum events, nature activities, Disney/Hello Kitty events, public events, community gatherings. Prioritize free/low-cost options for families with young kids (ages 1-4).

Step 2: Take the top 3-5 best events found and create Google Calendar entries for BOTH Saturday AND Sunday in the 'Ohana Suggestions' calendar. Use the calendar ID: 0a2b9ef123aed82993ef4b760cd766398a4c95c6b9d687b8cd968ceb27739c12@group.calendar.google.com

Use gws calendar events insert with --calendar-id flag to create each event. For each event include:
- A clear event title with '🎈' or '🎪' or '🌟' emoji prefix based on event type
- The location
- Start and end times (use reasonable times like 10am-12pm or 2pm-5pm depending on event type)
- A description with the source URL

Step 3: After creating the calendar events, send Raymond a brief Telegram message summarizing what was found and added to the calendar. List each event with its day (Sat/Sun), time, location, and brief description.

IMPORTANT: 
- Only create events that actually exist (don't make up fake events)
- If you cannot verify an event exists, skip it
- Use --description to add source URL to each event
- Confirm before creating events
Run /home/raymond/.openclaw/backup.sh and report if anything was backed up or if there were no changes.
